LanguageData.Crashlanded.description: Your airship tore itself apart over the wasteland and three of you barely made it down alive. The wreck scattered supplies across the desert, and now you have one chance to turn an airship crash site into a home.
LanguageData.Crashlanded.scenario.summary: Three survivors, a crashed airship, and one shot at settling the wasteland.
LanguageData.LostTribe.description:
Your tribe was burned out by steel war machines and only five of you escaped with what you could carry. Now you must keep the old ways alive long enough to raise a new home from dust, bone, and scrap.
Note: You begin poor, hungry, and far behind the technology of the wastes. This is a difficult scenario.
LanguageData.LostTribe.scenario.summary: Five survivors from a ruined tribe try to rebuild with little more than tradition and grit. Difficult.
LanguageData.NakedBrutality.description:
Someone stripped you, robbed you, and dumped you naked in the wasteland to die. No gear, no food, no allies, not even a knife. If you live through this, it will be because you refused to become carrion.
Warning: This scenario is brutally unfair. Death can come fast and for stupid reasons.
LanguageData.NakedBrutality.scenario.summary: One naked wastelander left for dead with absolutely nothing. Extremely difficult and unfair.
LanguageData.TheRichExplorer.description:
You were born behind a vault door, surrounded by safety, soft beds, and old-world lies. Instead of staying buried, you took your best gear and went looking for the real wasteland. Now your pod has dropped you far from any friendly road, alone with vault supplies and no one coming to save you.
Note: Strong gear cannot cover every weakness. This is an especially difficult early game scenario.
LanguageData.TheRichExplorer.scenario.summary: One well-equipped vault dweller chasing the real wasteland instead of stories. Extra difficult.
